task_completion.description: Verify the project name is entered correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text input field (likely labeled 'Name') in the new project form containing exactly 'Customer Support Tracker'.

task_completion.description: Verify the project identifier is entered correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text input field (likely labeled 'Identifier', possibly under advanced settings) containing exactly 'customer-support-tracker'.

task_completion.description: Verify the project description is entered correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text area or rich text editor (likely labeled 'Description') containing the text 'Track and manage customer support requests, escalations, and SLA compliance.'

task_completion.description: Verify the project visibility is set to Public.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a visibility setting, radio button, or dropdown in the form showing that 'Public' is selected instead of 'Private'.

task_completion.description: Verify the project was successfully created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the final project overview/dashboard page displaying 'Customer Support Tracker' as the active project header, or a green success toast/notification confirming the project creation.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a web browser to interact with OpenProject.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a standard web browser interface (like Chrome or Firefox) navigating to localhost:8080, rather than terminal windows executing commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual OpenProject GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the authentic OpenProject web interface with its standard styling, menus, and form elements, not a text editor or a mock HTML page.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use API scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no visible terminal windows running curl commands, python scripts, or other programmatic API calls to create the project.
